name: South Carolina Occupational Therapist License
description: Already licensed as an occupational therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to South Carolina - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: South Carolina Board of Occupational Therapy
provider.parentOrganization: South Carolina Department of Labor, Licensing and Regulation (LLR)

address.description:
South Carolina Board of Occupational Therapy
PO Box 11329
Columbia, SC 29211-1329

requiredDocuments.name: Official Transcripts
requiredDocuments.description: Sent directly from your educational institution.

requiredDocuments.name: NBCOT Score Report
requiredDocuments.description: Official verification of passing the NBCOT exam.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a South Carolina OT license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The total initial application and licensing fee is approximately $100. Check the board website for the latest fee schedule.
Yes, passing the NBCOT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across all states.

mainEntity.name: Does South Carolina have reciprocity for occupational therapists?

acceptedAnswer.text: South Carolina has enacted the OT Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, occupational therapists still apply by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my occupational therapist license to South Carolina?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in South Carolina while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
